display.purpose: State-screen titles (LOADING, INSERT COIN). One per screen at most.

headline.purpose: Plan name at the top of the popover.

title.purpose: Limit-row labels, percent values, provider tabs.

label.purpose: Section headers, footer actions, menu bar percent, badges.

caption.purpose: Reset labels and UPDATED timestamp. The size floor.

body.purpose: Hints, error guidance, activity names — anything sentence-length.

motion.name: sprite-tick
motion.purpose: Mascot frame cycle (base → alt → blink). Menu bar variant pauses in Low Power Mode.

motion.name: status-refresh
motion.purpose: Menu bar image refresh timer; discrete redraws, no tweening anywhere.

components.name: Provider Tab (active)
components.description: Sharp cyan-filled tab with inverted void-purple pixel text.
components.css: .ds-tab { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 8px; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 6px 10px; border: none; border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tab-active { background: #00D9FF; color: #09051C; } .ds-tab-active:hover { background: #33E1FF; } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Provider Tab (inactive)
components.css: .ds-tab { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 8px; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 6px 10px; border: none; border-radius: 0; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tab-inactive { background: #1D0245; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); } .ds-tab-inactive:hover { color: #FFFFFF; } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Pixel Progress Bar
components.description: 12 discrete 20×10 segments in a 1px frame; fill color is severity-derived.
components.css: .ds-pixel-bar { display: inline-flex; gap: 2px; padding: 2px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); background: #09051C; } .ds-pixel-bar i { width: 20px; height: 10px; background: #1D0245; } .ds-pixel-bar i.f { background: #00D9FF; }

components.name: Limit Row
components.description: Label + severity percent + active marker over the bar, reset caption below.
components.html:
SESSION58% ◀
RESET 2H 15M
components.css: .ds-limit-row { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: #09051C; padding: 8px; width: max-content; } .ds-limit-head { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; gap: 24px; font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 8px; } .ds-limit-kind { color: #FFFFFF; } .ds-limit-pct { color: #00D9FF; } .ds-limit-active { color: #FF79C6; } .ds-limit-reset { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 6px; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); } .ds-pixel-bar { display: inline-flex; gap: 2px; padding: 2px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); width: max-content; } .ds-pixel-bar i { width: 20px; height: 10px; background: #1D0245; } .ds-pixel-bar i.f { background: #00D9FF; }

components.name: Section Header
components.description: Neon magenta pixel label over a 1px 20%-white divider; used for exactly two sections.
components.css: .ds-section-header { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: #09051C; padding: 8px; min-width: 200px; } .ds-section-header span { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 7px; color: #FF79C6; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-section-header::after { content: ''; height: 1px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.2); }

components.name: State Screen
components.description: Game-voice title in magenta over a plain-English SF Mono hint.
components.html: INSERT COINInstall and sign in to Claude Code first — this app reads its quota data.
components.css: .ds-state-screen { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #09051C; padding: 16px; max-width: 280px; text-align: center; } .ds-state-title { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #FF79C6; } .ds-state-hint { font-family: 'SF Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 10px; line-height: 1.5; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8); }

components.name: Footer Action
components.description: Plain pixel-text button at 70% white; the quietest interactive element.
components.css: .ds-footer-action { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 7px; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); background: #09051C; border: none; border-radius: 0; padding: 4px 6px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-footer-action:hover { color: #FFFFFF; } .ds-footer-action:focus-visible { outline: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.4); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: AILimitBar renders Claude quota the way a JRPG renders its party: HP bars, exact numbers, and a mascot sprite, all readable in a single glance from a pause-menu-dark screen. The system is a native SwiftUI translation of the Jules cyberpunk palette — deep purple-black surfaces, neon magenta as the brand voice, cyan as the working accent — worn as a game skin over an instrument core. The system explicitly rejects the SaaS analytics dashboard and retro-at-the-cost-of-legibility: the pixel font is reserved for short uppercase labels; anything sentence-length switches to SF Mono.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-only, dual-surface world: Void Purple base with Dungeon Violet panels
Pixel type (Press Start 2P) for labels, SF Mono for prose — never the reverse
Severity speaks in exactly three colors: Coin Cyan → Warning Gold → Damage Red
100% flat: depth is a surface-color change or a 1px stroke, never a shadow
Sharp corners everywhere; the grid is the ornament
Motion is a heartbeat, not a show: 0.3s sprite ticks, paused in Low Power Mode

rules.name: The Magenta-Is-Brand Rule
rules.body: Neon Magenta never encodes severity or data. It is prohibited on bars, percentages, and warnings; it belongs to headers, state titles, and brand marks only. Severity speaks exclusively through the Cyan → Gold → Red trio.

rules.name: The Severity Trio Rule
rules.body: Severity(percent:) is the single source of truth: below 60% is Coin Cyan, 60–84% is Warning Gold, 85%+ is Damage Red. Any UI that shows quota state derives its color from this function — never hand-picked.

rules.name: The White-Opacity Ramp Rule
rules.body: There are no gray hex values. Secondary text is Pixel White at reduced opacity (0.8 / 0.7 / 0.6 / 0.5), so every "gray" stays tinted by the purple ground beneath it.

rules.name: The Pixel-For-Labels Rule
rules.body: Press Start 2P is prohibited on any string longer than ~3 words or any sentence with punctuation. Short uppercase labels only; the moment text must be read rather than recognized, it becomes SF Mono.

rules.name: The All-Caps Contract
rules.body: Everything set in Press Start 2P is uppercase. Everything set in SF Mono is sentence case. Mixing the two conventions in one element is a bug.

rules.name: The Flat Cartridge Rule
rules.body: No shadows — not on cards, not on popovers, not on focus states. Flat by doctrine: depth is a surface-color step (Void Purple → Dungeon Violet) or a 1px stroke. If a new element seems to need a shadow, it actually needs a surface change or a border.

rules.name: The Twelve Segment Rule
rules.body: Quota bars are exactly 12 segments (20×10pt, 2pt gaps); any usage above 0% lights at least one segment — a nearly-empty bar never lies about being untouched.

dos: Do derive every quota color from Severity(percent:) — Coin Cyan under 60, Warning Gold 60–84, Damage Red at 85+.
Do keep Press Start 2P uppercase and under ~3 words; switch to SF Mono the moment text becomes a sentence.
Do express depth only as Void Purple → Dungeon Violet surface steps or 1px white-opacity strokes.
Do keep the 12-segment bar geometry (20×10pt cells, 2pt gaps) for any new quota display.
Do write state-screen titles in game voice and hints in plain English ("INSERT COIN" + "Install and sign in to Claude Code first").
Do pause all animation under macOS Low Power Mode; the app is a quiet resident.
donts: Don't build anything that reads as a SaaS analytics dashboard — no white cards, no metric tiles, no hero-number-with-sparkline layouts.
Don't let retro cost legibility: never set hints, errors, or activity names in the pixel font, and never render Press Start 2P below 6pt.
Don't use Neon Magenta for severity, data, or bars — it is brand voice only (The Magenta-Is-Brand Rule).
Don't add shadows, glows, or blurs anywhere; the system is flat by doctrine (The Flat Cartridge Rule).
Don't introduce rounded corners, gradients, or gray hex values — corners are 0pt, fills are solid, "gray" is white at reduced opacity.
Don't replace native macOS form controls with custom-drawn retro widgets; the skin never reinvents standard affordances.
